description: Provides enhanced terminal accessibility for Windows Terminal and PowerShell, enabling screen reader users to efficiently navigate and interact with command-line interfaces. Inspired by TDSR (Terminal Data Structure Reader) and incorporates functionality from both TDSR and Speakup. Advanced features inspired by community suggestions and discussions.
changelog:
v2.3.0 collects everything since 2.0.3.

New: the buffer window. Press NVDA+Enter in a terminal to open the whole scrollback as a browsable snapshot: arrow through it, press H to move between the commands you ran and the errors they produced, follow the table of contents, read columnar output as real tables, and activate web links. NVDA+Shift+Enter opens a jump to line dialog that moves the review cursor to any line, found by its text. In the command layer, Shift+E shows only errors and Shift+C only the commands you ran.

Also new: any terminal NVDA recognizes is supported, not just those on a built-in list, so terminals the add-on used to ignore now work. Find reaches the scrollback on the legacy Windows console, where NVDA can only read the visible screen. And there is an optional word echo for terminals.

Fixed: braille follows the cursor again. Typing did not update the display until you panned away and back, and it never jumped back to the cursor. Key echo also no longer requires turning NVDA's own typing settings off; inside terminals it replaces them.

Security: passwords are no longer spoken by key echo. Characters are masked and completed words are never spoken while the terminal marks input as protected.

Laptop keyboard layout: NVDA+Enter is NVDA's activate-navigator-object command; Terminal Access takes it only inside terminals. Use the command layer's Enter, or reassign in Input Gestures, if you need NVDA's command there.

Requires NVDA 2025.1 or later. Existing settings are preserved on upgrade.

translations.displayName: NVDAのターミナルアクセス
translations.description: Windows TerminalおよびPowerShell向けに強化されたターミナルアクセシビリティを提供し、スクリーンリーダー利用者がコマンドラインインターフェースを効率的に操作・対話できるようにします。TDSR（Terminal Data Structure Reader）に着想を得て、TDSRとSpeakupの両方の機能を統合。コミュニティからの提案や議論を基に開発された高度な機能を備えています。

translations.description: Zapewnia lepszą dostępność terminala dla Windows Terminal i PowerShell, umożliwiając użytkownikom czytników ekranu sprawną nawigację i interakcję z interfejsami wiersza poleceń. Zainspirowany TDSR (Terminal Data Structure Reader) i łączący w sobie funkcje zarówno TDSR, jak i Speakup. Zaawansowane funkcje zainspirowane sugestiami i dyskusjami społeczności.

translations.displayName: 終端存取功能適用於 NVDA
translations.description: 為 Windows 終端機與 PowerShell 提供增強的終端機無障礙功能，使螢幕閱讀器使用者能高效導覽並與命令列介面互動。本工具受 TDSR（終端機資料結構閱讀器）啟發，整合了 TDSR 與 Speakup 的功能。進階特性源自社群建議與討論。

Inspirováno TDSR (Terminal Data Structure Reader) a zahrnuje funkce jak z TDSR, tak ze Speakup. Pokročilé funkce inspirované návrhy a diskusemi komunity.

translations.description: Fokozott terminál-hozzáférhetőséget biztosít a Windows Terminal és a PowerShell számára, lehetővé téve a képernyőolvasó felhasználók számára a parancssori felületeken való hatékony navigációt és interakciót. A TDSR (Terminal Data Structure Reader) ihlette, és mind a TDSR, mind a Speakup funkcióit magában foglalja. A közösség javaslatai és megbeszélései ihlette fejlett funkciók.

translations.description: Windows 터미널 및 PowerShell의 향상된 터미널 접근성을 제공하여, 스크린 리더 사용자가 명령줄 인터페이스를 효율적으로 탐색하고 상호작용할 수 있도록 합니다. TDSR(터미널 데이터 구조 리더)에서 영감을 받았으며 TDSR과 Speakup의 기능을 모두 통합합니다. 커뮤니티 제안 및 토론에서 영감을 받은 고급 기능을 제공합니다.

Press NVDA+Enter in a terminal to open the whole scrollback as a browsable snapshot: arrow through it, press H to move between the commands you ran and the errors they produced, follow the table of contents, read columnar output as real tables, and activate web links. NVDA+Shift+Enter opens a jump to line dialog that moves the review cursor to any line, found by its text. In the command layer, Shift+E shows only errors and Shift+C only the commands you ran.

Also new: any terminal NVDA recognizes is supported, not just those on a built-in list, so terminals the add-on used to ignore now work. Find reaches the scrollback on the legacy Windows console, where NVDA can only read the visible screen. And there is an optional word echo for terminals.

Fixed: braille follows the cursor again. Typing did not update the display until you panned away and back, and it never jumped back to the cursor. Key echo also no longer requires turning NVDA's own typing settings off; inside terminals it replaces them.

Security: passwords are no longer spoken by key echo. Characters are masked and completed words are never spoken while the terminal marks input as protected.

Laptop keyboard layout: NVDA+Enter is NVDA's activate-navigator-object command; Terminal Access takes it only inside terminals. Use the command layer's Enter, or reassign in Input Gestures, if you need NVDA's command there.

Requires NVDA 2025.1 or later. Existing settings are preserved on upgrade.

translations.description: Biedt verbeterde terminaltoegankelijkheid voor Windows Terminal en PowerShell, waardoor gebruikers van schermlezers efficiënt kunnen navigeren en communiceren met opdrachtregelinterfaces. Geïnspireerd door TDSR (Terminal Data Structure Reader) en bevat functionaliteit van zowel TDSR als Speakup. Geavanceerde functies geïnspireerd door suggesties en discussies uit de gemeenschap.

NVDA+Shift+Enter opens a jump to line dialog that moves the review cursor to any line, found by its text. In the command layer, Shift+E shows only errors and Shift+C only the commands you ran.

Also new: any terminal NVDA recognizes is supported, not just those on a built-in list, so terminals the add-on used to ignore now work. Find reaches the scrollback on the legacy Windows console, where NVDA can only read the visible screen. And there is an optional word echo for terminals.

Fixed: braille follows the cursor again. Typing did not update the display until you panned away and back, and it never jumped back to the cursor. Key echo also no longer requires turning NVDA's own typing settings off; inside terminals it replaces them.

Security: passwords are no longer spoken by key echo. Characters are masked and completed words are never spoken while the terminal marks input as protected.

Laptop keyboard layout: NVDA+Enter is NVDA's activate-navigator-object command; Terminal Access takes it only inside terminals. Use the command layer's Enter, or reassign in Input Gestures, if you need NVDA's command there.

Requires NVDA 2025.1 or later. Existing settings are preserved on upgrade.

translations.displayName: الوصول إلى المحطة الطرفية لـ NVDA
translations.description: يوفر إمكانية وصول محسّنة إلى Windows Terminal و PowerShell، مما يتيح لمستخدمي قارئ الشاشة التنقل والتفاعل بكفاءة مع واجهات سطر الأوامر. مستوحى من TDSR (Terminal Data Structure Reader) ويضم وظائف من كل من TDSR و Speakup. ميزات متقدمة مستوحاة من اقتراحات ومناقشات المجتمع.
